original: Eighth recorded perihelion passage of Halley's Comet

Ottoman soldiers lay siege to the Armenian city of Van during World War I
The Armenians rise and seize the Turkish town of Van, which they hold until Russians relieve them on 19 May; thousands of Armenians are killed

OAS-leader ex-general Salan arrested in Algiers
-30] All Africa Conferences of Churches opens in Kampala Uganda

Bombs planted by Loyalists members of the Ulster Volunteer Force and the Ulster Protestant Volunteers explode at Silent Valley reservoir in County Down and at an electricity pylon at Kilmore, County Armagh

Kallicharran scores his 2nd Test century in his 2nd Test Cricket

Karyn Marshall of NYC lifted 303 lbs in a clean & jerk lift

Twelve mortar rounds are fired on Abu Ghraib Prison in Iraq by insurgents, killing 22 detainees and wounding 92
A man with a handgun barricades himself in NASA's Johnson Space Center in Houston, Texas, before killing a male hostage and then himself

40 people are killed and 27 injured after a tractor trailer collided with a bus in Alamo, Mexico
Plane crash near Islamabad, Pakistan, kills 127 people
193 people are killed and 11,826 are injured after a magnitude 7.0 earthquake strikes Lushan County, China

Giorgio Napolitano is re-elected President of Italy

136 people massacred in village of Karma, Burkina Faso, one of the country worst attacks on civilians, blamed on the country's security forces
Total solar eclipse in Exeter, Western Australia blocks out the sun for 58 seconds

Only documented performance ofWilliam Shakespeare's tragedy "Macbeth" during the playwright's lifetime, at the Globe Theatre, London, in notes reviewing the play by astrologer Simon Forman

Oliver Cromwelland 40 musketeers forcibly dissolve the English Rump Parliament, after it failed to establish a caretaker government. In Cromwell's words "You have sat too long for any good you have been doing lately ... In the name of God, go!"
The former KingJames IIof England, now deposed, lays siege to Derry
Nicholas Rowe's play "The Tragedy of LadyJane Grey" premieres in London

President of Chad for three decades,Idriss Débyis killed on a battlefield fighting rebels near the capital, N'Djamena
